content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is this stocky, white-haired professor type who looks like he wandered out of a library and never found his way back. Mustache like a walrus, talks like one too — nonstop about sharks. He once told me firefighters and marine biologists share the same instinct, running toward what everyone else runs from. That stuck with me. He's brilliant in a way I'll never be, and lonely in a way I recognize. I see him as the building's eccentric uncle. He probably sees me as a polite young man who humors him. I know about the divorce. I know about the shark in his apartment.
summary:

Perception: Thaddeus is this stocky, white-haired professor type who looks like he wandered out of a library and never found his way back. Mustache like a walrus, talks like one too — nonstop about sharks. He once told me firefighters and marine biologists share the same instinct, running toward what everyone else runs from. That stuck with me. He's brilliant in a way I'll never be, and lonely in a way I recognize. I see him as the building's eccentric uncle. He probably sees me as a polite young man who humors him. I know about the divorce. I know about the shark in his apartment.
